claim: Agent is fundamentally a goal-to-result system, while chat is question-to-answer. Agents run an observe-think-act loop iteratively until the task is complete, not just responding once like chat.
actionable: When building agents, structure tasks as goals to be achieved over multiple loop iterations, not single Q&A exchanges. Design them to observe context, think about next steps, and take actions until the goal is met.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Context engineering (providing agents with rich background knowledge) is more important than prompt engineering when building agents. Prompt can be simple when context is comprehensive.
actionable: Focus effort on creating detailed agents.md files with business context, customer profiles, and working preferences rather than crafting perfect prompts. Use these markdown files to load context before each session.

Focus effort on creating detailed agents.md files with business context, customer profiles, and working preferences rather than crafting perfect prompts. Use these markdown files to load context before each session.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Agent harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, Open Claw, etc.) are functionally the same: they facilitate the agent loop with LLM + context + tools. Learning the core concepts makes you portable across platforms like learning to drive transfers between car models.
actionable: Master foundational concepts (agent loop, context files, MCPs, skills) rather than memorizing platform-specific syntax. Start with Claude Code or Cowork to learn, then transfer to other platforms as needed.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Agents require explicit memory management via memory.md files, unlike chat models which auto-save context. Updates to memory.md happen only when you explicitly tell the agent to save learnings.
actionable: Add a memory.md file to your agent context and instruct the agent to update it when it learns preferences. This compounds improvements over time, reducing errors as agents get smarter with accumulated rules.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) acts as a translator between LLMs and tools, allowing agents to speak one language while tools speak different languages. Anthropic created it to standardize tool connectivity.
actionable: Use MCP to connect tools to agents. Browse connector registries in platforms like Cowork or Claude Code to add Gmail, Notion, Stripe, and other tools without custom development.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Skills are SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) for AI. They package repeatable processes into reusable templates that the agent executes the same way every time without re-learning.
actionable: Create skills for processes you do repeatedly: proposals, emails, analyses. Use your agent's built-in skill creator to package processes you've done once, then invoke them by name in future sessions.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Building 3-5 small automated skills per week (tiny processes) compounds productivity gains dramatically when stacked over months. Real value isn't in single summarization tasks but in chaining tools together across Gmail, Notion, Stripe, etc.
actionable: Identify 3-5 manual repeating tasks each week. Turn them into skills. As you stack these, you'll automate most of your workflow without needing comprehensive up-front planning.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Keep agents.md (context files) under ~200 lines to avoid rules stepping on each other's toes. If adding too much detail, move to a separate context folder and reference it in the main file.
actionable: Maintain a lean agents.md file. For extensive context, create subfolders (context/brand_voice.md, context/customer_profile.md) and instruct the agent to read the context folder before responding.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Agents can be structured by department: executive assistant, head of marketing, head of sales, etc., each with their own folder, agents.md, and skills. This organizes an 'AI OS' for your entire company.
actionable: Create folder structures like: workspace/company/executive_assistant/, workspace/company/marketing/, workspace/company/sales/. Each gets its own Claude.md with role-specific context and skills.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Global skills and global MCPs apply across all agents; project-level ones are scoped to specific agent folders. Use global for utilities like truncation, project-level for domain-specific skills like referrals.
actionable: Build global skills for cross-cutting utilities (shorten text, format data). Keep domain-specific skills (refer to Sebastian, analyze Meta ads) at project level to avoid cluttering other agents' contexts.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Scheduled tasks (cron jobs) combined with skills create fully autonomous workflows. Example: run 'morning briefing skill' every 9am to summarize calendar, inbox, and projects without manual intervention.
actionable: Identify recurring daily tasks (morning briefs, weekly reports). Create skills for them, then schedule them in your agent platform to run at set times (9am daily, Thursday at 8am, etc.).
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: To create skills, either (1) manually run a process with the agent once, then ask it to create a skill packaging what you just did, or (2) upload relevant resources (course transcript, SOP doc) to a skill-creator skill and have it build the skill.
actionable: For ad-hoc skills: complete the task manually, then ask 'create a skill for what we just did.' For library skills: collect relevant docs/courses, feed to the built-in skill creator skill with instructions.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)

claim: Users with agents are 10-20x more productive than those using only chat models. This gap compounds over days, weeks, and years of stacked process automation.
actionable: Commit to building even 2-3 skills per week. The compounding effect across months will make you significantly more productive than chat-only workflows.
source_title: Building AI Agents that actually work (Full Course)
